free_text: Elastic scattering of polarized protons by Li-6.
LiF on a backing of Ni was used, which caused problems
because of interfering lines from inelastic scattering
at F and Ni. These lines could be removed by using a
sandwich target Al-6Li-C, and, with further improvement
As the exact determination of the target thicknesses of
the 6Li targets was not practicable, the overall
normalization of the absolute cross section was gotten
by adjusting our data between 1.8 and 2.8 MeV to
steps of 10 degr on two rotatable rings, which were
turned by 5 degr. between two runs.
5% on overall normalization error from MONIT-REF
Data for the energy range of 7.5-10.0 MeV
were added. Low case in "AUTHOR" text.
Titles of digitizing errors changed according to Dict.
SF6=SIG in REACTION code changed in Subent 003.
total elastic cross-section without Coulomb interaction
Cr.sect. has been calculated in a phase-shift analysis,
SD: SF6=SIG in REACTION code changed on IPA
